title: surenus glaziovii
side_effects: Surenus glaziovii can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing allergic reactions, triggering asthma attacks, or even causing respiratory failure in severe cases. Prolonged exposure to its extracts can also lead to skin irritation, including redness, itching, and rashes. Ingestion of the plant's leaves or seeds can cause nausea, vomiting, and stomach ulcers due to its toxic compounds. Additionally, improper use of Surenus glaziovii may also interact with other medications, resulting in adverse effects such as increased blood pressure or heart rate.
